calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kerry Riordan for and with May Mok (Petitioner).

Ms. Riordan informed the Court that she filed four waivers of notice on behalf of the heirs at law (Kimberley Mok, Samantha Mok, May Mok on her own behalf, and May Mok as the natural guardian of the minor). Ms. Riordan provided the Court with a copy of the fourth supplemental declaration and pointed out that Exhibit L is the bank statement for the small cash Schwab account from February 1, 2012 through February 29, 2012. Ms. Riordan also stated that the Petitioner is withdrawing her request to have the two pieces of real property as community property, stated that funds in the retirement account were comingled and that the institution has no way of differentiating funds, that the PG&E stocks were purchased during marriage and that the dividends checks were being deposited into the joint checking account (shown as Ex. K on the third supplement). Ms. Mok stated that all four family members discussed the matter. The Petition for Spousal Property was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Continued To Apr-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Continued To Mar-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kerry Riordan Sykes for and with May Mok.

Ms. Sykes addressed the accounts that were in held joint tenancy as well as the accounts that were held in the Decedent's name, explained that the retirement account from St. Mary's was Lincoln Financial and then changed to Fidelity, that the forms to transfer the account were not signed, that Mr. Chung set up the Fidelity account but did not transfer the account and that he contributed for two and a half years prior to the marriage to Ms. Mok. Ms. Sykes also spoke of a safe deposit box, Ms. Mok not having access to it and Ms. Mok not knowing what the content was. The Court stated that it needs more detail about the source of the funds, the source of the retirement funds, whether the funds used to purchase the house were Trust funds and what happened to those Trust distributions. The Petition for Spousal Property was continued to March 20, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a supplemental declaration.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
